name: Electromagnetism
description: Maxwell's equations, electromagnetic waves, optics, and classical field theory. The unification of electricity, magnetism, and light into a single classical field. From the glow of a lightbulb to the propagation of radio waves, electromagnetism governs all phenomena involving charged particles and electromagnetic fields.
axioms: Gauss's law: ∇·E = ρ/ε₀ (electric charges produce diverging electric fields)
Gauss's law for magnetism: ∇·B = 0 (no magnetic monopoles exist)
Faraday's law: ∇×E = -∂B/∂t (a changing magnetic field induces an electric field)
Ampère-Maxwell law: ∇×B = μ₀J + μ₀ε₀∂E/∂t (currents and changing electric fields produce magnetic fields)
Lorentz force: F = q(E + v×B) — the force on a charged particle in electromagnetic fields
Electromagnetic waves travel at c = 1/√(μ₀ε₀) in vacuum — light is EM radiation
Charge is conserved: ∂ρ/∂t + ∇·J = 0 (continuity equation, follows from Maxwell's equations)
reasoning_patterns: Boundary value problems: solve Laplace/Poisson equation with boundary conditions for E and V
Multipole expansion: approximate distant charge/current distributions by monopole, dipole, quadrupole terms
Retarded potentials: account for finite propagation speed using Lienard-Wiechert potentials
Gauge transformation: exploit freedom in A and φ to simplify calculations (Coulomb gauge, Lorenz gauge)
Symmetry exploitation: cylindrical, spherical, planar symmetry reduces vector PDEs to scalar ODEs
Method of images: replace boundary conditions with fictitious charges to solve Laplace's equation
Faraday's law application: changing magnetic flux induces EMF in loops (generators, transformers)
boundary_conditions: Classical EM breaks down at quantum scales — needs quantum electrodynamics (QED)
Does not cover optical coherence theory or quantum optics in depth
Does not cover antenna engineering or microwave engineering (applied domains)
Radiation reaction and self-force on accelerating charges are problematic classically (Abraham-Lorentz force)
Classical EM has no explanation for photon discreteness — requires QED for photoelectric effect
key_references: Feynman, R. The Feynman Lectures on Physics Vol.2
Landau & Lifshitz. The Classical Theory of Fields (Vol.2)
Maxwell, J.C. (1865). A Dynamical Theory of the Electromagnetic Field
canonical_truths: Maxwell's equations unify electricity, magnetism, and optics into a single theory
Electromagnetic waves propagate at c — light IS electromagnetic radiation
Charge is conserved (∂ρ/∂t + ∇·J = 0) — follows from Maxwell's equations
The speed of light is the same in all inertial frames — the central clue leading to special relativity
Electromagnetic energy density: u = ½(ε₀E² + B²/μ₀); Poynting vector S = E×H gives energy flux
Maxwell's equations are not invariant under Galilean transformation — they motivated Lorentz invariance
